D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

CIE94 — Chroma Loosens the Tolerance

push the reference chroma up and watch the same colour shift count for less, because SC grows with C₁

CIE94 keeps the same shift but divides the chroma and hue terms by SC = 1 + K₁·C₁ and SH = 1 + K₂·C₁. The more saturated the reference, the larger those denominators — so a vivid colour tolerates a bigger error than a pale one.

Raise the reference chroma C₁ and hold the colour shift fixed. The SC, SH meters stretch and ΔE94 falls — while the plain ΔE76 (no weighting) stays put. Toggle the graphic-arts and textile parameter sets.
